title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What is a Taxpayer identification number?
Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN), is a unique number given to businesses and even individuals enrolled under the erstwhile regime of Value Added Tax or VAT. In fact, all businesses and individuals must have to mention their unique TIN on all transactions related to VAT. The same is crucial in the administration of value-added tax. ¹ But what makes TIN important? Lets find out.
What is Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN)?
Before going any further, let's first start with the basics. What is Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN)? Well, TIN or taxpayer identification number is an 11-digit special code provided to all businesses and entities enrolled under the (erstwhile) regime of VAT.¹ The purpose behind the same is very simple. It comes in handy for tax authorities to track and monitor taxpayers. Thus, it proves to be quite beneficial in the overall administration of taxes.
It is mandatory for all businesses or entities engaged in trading goods and services to have their Taxpayer Identification Number TIN. ¹
The Taxpayer Identification Number TIN. format is unique for each state in India. The general format has a distinct state code in the beginning, followed by some letters and numbers.
Why do you need TIN?
Having your unique TIN isquite important for all taxpayers registered under VAT. The TIN has replaced the Central Sales Tax (CST) number or sales tax numbers
Having a TIN facilitates the purchase and sale of goods and services at both levels: within the state and between different states. In addition to this, the TIN generated by the commercial tax department helps greatly in recording and tracking transactions of businesses under VAT. Therefore, creating a tax system that is accountable and transparent.
Who Is Required to Obtain a TIN in India?
As said earlier, in India, a TIN number is required for all entities involved in collecting or deducting taxes, such as businesses registered under VAT, CST. Individuals or organizations making taxable sales, purchases, or deductions must obtain tax ID number. The tax ID number acts as a unique identifier to monitor tax compliance and link transactions with the Income Tax Department.
What is Importance of TIN Number?
A taxpayer identification number (TIN) is important because it acts as a unique identification for tracking tax-related activities of individuals and businesses. It helps the government monitor sales, purchases, and deductions, ensuring proper tax collection. For businesses, the tax ID number builds credibility, ensures proper compliance with tax laws and allows interstate trade, without a valid tax ID number, filing for taxes and smooth business operations becomes difficult.
How do you apply for your taxpayer identification number?
You can apply for taxpayer identification number online and offline.
The online procedure for the TIN application is not only super easy but also takes less time and effort. Here is how you may obtain your taxpayer identification number online
Step 1: Visit the Official Tax Portal
Visit the official website of the state's commercial tax department or VAT portal.
Step 2: Complete the Registration Form
Complete the registration on the portal by duly filling in your details
Step 3: Upload the Required Documents
Once you're done providing the mandatory information, complete your form by adding the essential documents.
Step 4: Submit the Application
Once the form is complete and documents are uploaded, submit your registration application through the portal.
Step 5: Verification by the Department
After submitting your registration application, the state verifies your details and the overall form.
Step 6 : Issuance of Taxpayer Identification Number
If verification is successful, the commercial tax department initiates a unique TIN for you and you will get a provisional registration certificate.
Step 1: Visit the Official Website
Visit the official website of the commercial tax department of the respective State.
Step 2: Download the Application Form
Download and print the registration application from the official website.
Step 3: Fill in Your Details
Fill in all the necessary details and complete the same with your signature.
Step 4 : Attach Required Documents
Attach all the essential documents and sign the form then send it to your nearest facilitation point.
Step 5: Verification
The authorities will verify your registration form.
Step 6: Issuance of Taxpayer Identification Number
Once your application is successfully verified, the commercial tax department will give you a unique TAN and your VAT certificate.
Essential documents for your TIN application
As mentioned, your TIN or Taxpayer identification number is generated by the commercial tax department of the respective states. This means different states may ask for different documents to be attached to your TIN registration form. However, there are some general documents that you must keep in handy to ensure you fill your form smoothly and hassle-free.
Here is a list of such documents.1
Your identity proof
Your proof of residence or address
Proprietor’s PAN card
Proprietor's passport-size pictures
Some sort of security or reference documents
Certificate of incorporation of the business entity.
Memorandum of association and articles and association in the case of companies registered under the Companies Act.
While you upload these documents, it is important to make sure that all the documents are up-to-date and accurate.
How to Know the TIN Application Status1?
You can easily track your TIN application status online through the official website of your state’s commercial tax department. Login to the portal and you will find a dedicated section for “TIN Application Status” where you just need to enter your application number, acknowledgment number and it will show the status of TIN.
State-Wise Code List in India: TIN First Two Digits1
Difference Between TIN and GSTIN
Key Takeaways
A Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) is a unique ID for tracking taxes of businesses and individuals.
Businesses can apply for a TIN through the state commercial tax department, either online or offline.
A TIN or tax ID number ensures compliance, transparency, and proper taxpayer identification.
In India, TIN is mainly used under the VAT laws, but with GST implementation, it has been major replaced by GSTIN, except for goods and services not registered under GST
Conclusion
Having TIN isn’t just a legal obligation for businesses; it's much more than that. The unique code ensures compliance with VAT, effective tax administration, helps you with your VAT returns, and improves your business’s transparency and credibility too. Once you understand the importance of your unique taxpayer identification number, you can easily obtain it online or offline. With a few easy online steps, you can easily download your unique TIN and VAT certificate.
FAQs
Are TIN and PAN the same?
No, TIN and PAN are not the same. TIN, or Taxpayer identification number, is an 11-digit unique code that is significant with VAT-related regulations. However, a PAN or Permanent account number is a 10-digit alphanumeric number issued to each taxpayer which is also used to check on financial transactions that carry a taxable component..2
Where can I find my TIN?
In India, there are several ways to find your TIN or taxpayer identification number. One of the ways is to check   online portals provided by tax authorities in various jurisdictions.3
What is Format of TIN Number?
The format of a TIN number in India is an 11-digit unique identification number. The first two digits represent the state code, and the remaining nine digits are unique to the registered business or individual.
Are TIN and TAN the Same in India?
No, TIN and TAN are not same. While TIN is allocated by commercial tax department to keep track all VAT related activities, TAN is issued by income tax department to streamline the deduction and collection of TDS.
What is TIN in income tax?
In income tax, a TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number) is an 11-digit number issued to businesses registered under VAT to track sales and tax payments.
How do I find my TIN?
You can find your TIN (taxpayer identification number) on your VAT registration certificate. You can also find your TIN number online by visiting your state’s commercial tax web portal.
Disclaimer
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Co. Ltd. assumes no liability for its accuracy. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility

What is a Taxpayer identification number?
Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN), is a unique number given to businesses and even individuals enrolled under the erstwhile regime of Value Added Tax or VAT. In fact, all businesses and individuals must have to mention their unique TIN on all transactions related to VAT. The same is crucial in the administration of value-added tax. ¹ But what makes TIN important? Lets find out.
What is Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN)?
Before going any further, let's first start with the basics. What is Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN)? Well, TIN or taxpayer identification number is an 11-digit special code provided to all businesses and entities enrolled under the (erstwhile) regime of VAT.¹ The purpose behind the same is very simple. It comes in handy for tax authorities to track and monitor taxpayers. Thus, it proves to be quite beneficial in the overall administration of taxes.
It is mandatory for all businesses or entities engaged in trading goods and services to have their Taxpayer Identification Number TIN. ¹
The Taxpayer Identification Number TIN. format is unique for each state in India. The general format has
a distinct state code in the beginning, followed by some letters and numbers.
Why do you need TIN?
Having your unique TIN isquite important for all taxpayers registered under VAT. The TIN has
replaced the Central Sales Tax (CST) number or sales tax numbers
Having a TIN facilitates the purchase and sale of goods and services at both levels: within the state and
between different states. In addition to this, the TIN generated by the commercial tax department helps
greatly in recording and tracking transactions of businesses under VAT. Therefore, creating a tax system
that is accountable and transparent.
Who Is Required to Obtain a TIN in India?
As said earlier, in India, a TIN number is required for all entities involved in collecting or deducting taxes, such as businesses registered under VAT, CST . Individuals or organizations making taxable sales, purchases, or deductions must obtain tax ID number. The tax ID number acts as a unique identifier to monitor tax compliance and link transactions with the Income Tax Department.
What is Importance of TIN Number?
A taxpayer identification number (TIN) is important because it acts as a unique identification for tracking tax-related activities of individuals and businesses. It helps the government monitor sales, purchases, and deductions, ensuring proper tax collection. For businesses, the tax ID number builds credibility, ensures proper compliance with tax laws and allows interstate trade, without a valid tax ID number, filing for taxes and smooth business operations becomes difficult.
How do you apply for your taxpayer identification number?
You can apply for taxpayer identification number online and offline.
The online procedure for the TIN application is not only super easy but also takes less time and effort.
Here is how you may obtain your taxpayer identification number online
Step 1: Visit the Official Tax Portal
Visit the official website of the state's commercial tax department or VAT portal.
Step 2: Complete the Registration Form
Complete the registration on the portal by duly filling in your details
Step 3: Upload the Required Documents
Once you're done providing the mandatory information, complete your form by adding the essential documents.
Step 4: Submit the Application
Once the form is complete and documents are uploaded, submit your registration application through the portal.
Step 5: Verification by the Department
After submitting your registration application, the state verifies your details and the overall form.
Step 6 : Issuance of Taxpayer Identification Number
If verification is successful, the commercial tax department initiates a unique TIN for you and you will
get a provisional registration certificate.
If you wish to apply for TIN offiline, Here’s how you can get it done Step 1: Visit the Official Website
Visit the official website of the commercial tax department of the respective State.
Step 2: Download the Application Form
Download and print the registration application from the official website.
Step 3: Fill in Your Details
Fill in all the necessary details and complete the same with your signature.
Step 4 : Attach Required Documents
Attach all the essential documents and sign the form then send it to your nearest facilitation point.
Step 5: Verification
The authorities will verify your registration form.
Step 6: Issuance of Taxpayer Identification Number
Once your application is successfully verified, the commercial tax department will give you a unique TAN
and your VAT certificate.
Essential documents for your TIN application
As mentioned, your TIN or Taxpayer identification number is generated by the commercial tax department
of the respective states. This means different states may ask for different documents to be attached to
your TIN registration form. However, there are some general documents that you must keep in handy to
ensure you fill your form smoothly and hassle-free.
Here is a list of such documents.1
1. Your identity proof
2. Your proof of residence or address
3. Proprietor’s PAN card
4. Proprietor's passport-size pictures
5. Some sort of security or reference documents
6. Certificate of incorporation of the business entity.
7. Memorandum of association and articles and association in the case of companies registered
under the Companies Act.
While you upload these documents, it is important to make sure that all the documents are up-to-date
and accurate.
How to Know the TIN Application Status1?
You can easily track your TIN application status online through the official website of your state’s commercial tax department. Login to the portal and you will find a dedicated section for “TIN
Application Status” where you just need to enter your application number, acknowledgment number and it will show the status of TIN.
State-Wise Code List in India: TIN First Two Digits1
State / Union Territory TIN (first 2 digits)
Andaman and Nicobar Islands 35
Andhra Pradesh 28
Andhra Pradesh (New) 37
Arunachal Pradesh 12
Assam 18
Bihar 10
Chandigarh 04
Chhattisgarh 22
Dadra and Nagar Haveli 26
Daman and Diu 25
Delhi 07
Goa 30
Gujarat 24
Haryana 06
Himachal Pradesh 02
Jammu and Kashmir 01
Jharkhand 20
Karnataka 29
Kerala 32
Lakshadweep Islands 31
Madhya Pradesh 23
Maharashtra 27
Manipur 14
Meghalaya 17
Mizoram 15
Nagaland 13
Odisha 21
Pondicherry 34
Punjab 03
Rajasthan 08
Sikkim 11
Tamil Nadu 33
Telangana 36
Tripura 16
Uttar Pradesh 09
Uttarakhand 05
West Bengal 19
Difference Between TIN and GSTIN
Pointers TIN GSTIN
Meaning
TIN is a unique alphanumeric registration number assigned to businesses and companies that are liable to pay Value Added Tax (VAT).
A GSTIN is a unique identification number assigned to every dealer or service provider registered under the GST Act.
Full Form
Taxpayer Identification Number Goods and Services Tax Identification Number
Mandatory for
Any business or individual involved in the sale of goods and liable to pay VAT must have a TIN (Tax Identification Number).
Anyone engaged in the supply of goods or services with an annual turnover above the prescribed GST threshold must have a GSTIN.
Format
11-digit alpha numeric number allotted by the state commercial tax department
15-digit alphanumeric code allotted by GST authorities
Where to apply for it?
Either from your State’s sales tax department website or from the commercial tax website
www.gst.gov.in
Mode of registration
Online and offline Online
Applicable Law
Business registered under the Service Tax Act 2005
Goods and Services Tax (GST) Act, 2017
Current Status
GSTIN largely replaced TIN but it is still levied on certain goods like petroleum liquor etc. on which GST is not applicable
Mandatory for all GST- registered businesses
Key Takeaways
● A Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) is a unique ID for tracking taxes of businesses and individuals. ● Businesses can apply for a TIN through the state commercial tax department, either online or offline. ● A TIN or tax ID number ensures compliance, transparency, and proper taxpayer identification. ● In India, TIN is mainly used under the VAT laws, but with GST implementation, it has been major replaced by GSTIN, except for goods and services not registered under GST
Conclusion
Having TIN isn’t just a legal obligation for businesses; it's much more than that. The unique code ensures
compliance with VAT, effective tax administration, helps you with your VAT returns, and improves your
business’s transparency and credibility too. Once you understand the importance of your unique taxpayer
identification number, you can easily obtain it online or offline. With a few easy online steps, you can
easily download your unique TIN and VAT certificate.
FAQs
Are TIN and PAN the same?
No, TIN and PAN are not the same. TIN, or Taxpayer identification number, is an 11-digit unique code
that is significant with VAT-related regulations. However, a PAN or Permanent account number is a
10-digit alphanumeric number issued to each taxpayer which is also used to check on financial
transactions that carry a taxable component..2
Where can I find my TIN?
In India, there are several ways to find your TIN or taxpayer identification number. One of the ways is
to check online portals provided by tax authorities in various jurisdictions.3
What is Format of TIN Number?
The format of a TIN number in India is an 11-digit unique identification number. The first two digits represent the state code, and the remaining nine digits are unique to the registered business or individual.
Are TIN and TAN the Same in India?
No, TIN and TAN are not same. While TIN is allocated by commercial tax department to keep track all VAT related activities, TAN is issued by income tax department to streamline the deduction and collection of TDS .
What is TIN in income tax?
In income tax, a TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number) is an 11-digit number issued to businesses registered under VAT to track sales and tax payments.
How do I find my TIN?
You can find your TIN (taxpayer identification number) on your VAT registration certificate . You can also find your TIN number online by visiting your state’s commercial tax web portal.
